verdict_reason: v3.1 verification. The engineering layer continues to restrict itself to the consensus across Shankara (Advaita), Ramanuja (Vishishtadvaita), Mukundananda, and Prabhupada and does NOT take a sectarian position on the disputed scope of adhikāra (Shankara's jñāna-niṣṭhā contrast vs. Ramanuja's obligatory/occasional/desiderative-Vedic-act scope). The disagreement is correctly omitted from the engineering layer — appropriately, since this layer is the modern adaptation, not the source-pack. Crucially, the prior-pass concern about compression of the three second-hemistich prohibitions is now RESOLVED. v3.1 explicitly separates the three movements: 'First' renders karmaṇy eva adhikāras te / mā phaleṣu kadācana (right-in-action / no-claim-on-fruits), 'Second' now distinctly renders mā karma-phala-hetuḥ bhūḥ as 'do not regard yourself as the cause of those fruits ... To inwardly claim authorship of the result is to bind yourself as the agent of consequences you cannot control' — which is exactly the karma-phala-hetuḥ proper, not the saṅga-on-phala under-reading the prior pass flagged. This also tracks Shankara's specific gloss that desire for fruits binds the actor as the agent (kartā) of those results. 'Third' renders mā te saṅgaḥ astu akarmaṇi as the explicit rejection of inaction-as-retreat — tracking Ramanuja's emphasis that Arjuna's proposed inaction is rejected. Several technical terms are still rendered into modern English, but the calibration rule explicitly allows this when consensus across traditions is genuine, and consensus here is genuine. The 5.x and 18.66 reference in the counter-example correctly invokes the textual companion to the akarman question without misattributing it to a tradition. Cross-persona signal: RESOLVED. PASS.
